2604.02912.title: Corporations Constitute Intelligence
2604.02912.paper_summary: In January 2026, Anthropic published a 79-page "constitution" for its AI model Claude, the most comprehensive corporate AI governance document ever released. This Article offers the first legal and democratic-theoretic analysis of that document. Despite genuine philosophical sophistication, the constitution harbors two structural defects. First, it excludes the contexts where ethical constraints matter most: models deployed to the U.S. military operate under different rules, a gap exposed when Claude remained embedded in Palantir's Maven platform during military strikes in Iran even after a government-wide ban on Anthropic's technology. Second, its very comprehensiveness forecloses democratic contestation by resolving questions about AI values, moral status, and conscientious objection that should remain open for public deliberation. Anthropic's own 2023 experiment in participatory constitution-making found roughly 50% divergence between publicly sourced and corporate-authored principles, with the democratic version producing lower bias across nine social dimensions, yet the 2026 constitution incorporates none of those findings. I argue that AI governance suffers from a "political community deficit": the absence of any democratic body authorized to determine the principles governing AI behavior. Corporate transparency, however admirable, is not democratic legitimacy.
2604.02912.paper_summary_zh: 在2026年1月，Anthropic 發布了一份79頁的AI模型Claude的「憲法」，這是迄今為止最全面的企業AI治理文件。本文提供了對該文件的首次法律和民主理論分析。儘管具有真正的哲學深度，這部憲法卻存在兩個結構性缺陷。首先，它排除了倫理約束最為重要的情境：部署於美國軍方的模型遵循不同的規則，這一缺口在Claude在伊朗軍事打擊期間仍嵌入Palantir的Maven平台時暴露出來，即使在整個政府對Anthropic技術的禁令之後。其次，其過於全面的特性使得民主競爭無法進行，因為它解決了有關AI價值觀、道德地位和良心抗拒的問題，而這些問題應該保持開放以供公眾討論。Anthropic自己在2023年的參與式憲法制定實驗發現，公共來源和企業撰寫的原則之間存在約50%的差異，民主版本在九個社會維度上產生了較低的偏見，但2026年的憲法並未納入這些發現。我主張AI治理存在「政治社群赤字」：缺乏任何被授權決定AI行為原則的民主機構。然而，企業透明度再怎麼可嘉，也不是民主合法性。
2604.02912.author: Gilad Abiri et.al.
